highlights.text: The bull case tomorrow is simply that ES can fill this out more (entries discussed above) then breakout.
Again - my lean was they could.
My general lean is we can fill this structure out, perhaps trap it, then head to 6846, 6873, 6893, then 6903.” By noon today, we hit 6873.
Last Monday March 30th, something important happened in ES: It put in my core setup - the Failed Breakdown.
As I frequently discuss all major rallies in ES start on Failed Breakdowns, because Failed Breakdowns are how institutions accumulate .

We saw it last Monday March 30th as stated and this was a major accumulation event as institutions prepped for what ended up being a 480 point leg up - as always, we followed them : ES plunged to 6353 last Monday March 30th in evening going elevator down .
An alternative is to wait for it to flush and recover (perhaps to 6353) for a Failed Breakdown.” We got this trigger at 830pm last Monday evening, and were off to last weeks 6650 high.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow
Targets would be 6677, 6691, 6716, then 6742” By the close Tuesday, we hit 6663 range resistance, and broke out viciously to 6850 Tuesday morning.

post_title: Massive Breakout Underway For SPX. How Much More Upside? April 10 Plan
